mechanism_narrative: A rise in the housing market signal, as measured by HOUST, indicates a strengthening demand for goods and services, which should produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in housing market recoveries are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate signal, as measured by UNRATE, indicates a weakening labor market, which should produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in labor market downturns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the supply signal, as measured by INDPRO, indicates a weakening supply of goods and services, which should produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in supply-side contractions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the monetary policy signal, as measured by FEDFUNDS, indicates an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the labor force participation rate signal, as measured by PAYEMS, indicates a strengthening labor market, which should produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in labor market expansions are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the consumer price index signal, as measured by CPIAUCSL, indicates a weakening inflationary pressure, which should produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in inflationary eases are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the supply signal, as measured by RSAFS, indicates a strengthening supply of goods and services, which should produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in supply-side expansions are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the gross domestic product signal, as measured by GDPC1, indicates a weakening demand for goods and services, which should produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in demand-side contractions are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
